title: Yacht Rental in Tulum: A 2026 Guide to Costs and Best Boats
html:
For a luxury escape along the Riviera Maya, yacht rental in Tulum offers access to turquoise waters and hidden coves. Departing from Marina Puerto Aventuras, a half-day charter on a 40-foot motor yacht starts around €1,200 for up to eight guests. BoatHire24 connects you with verified operators to make planning straightforward.

Where to set off in Tulum

The main departure points are Marina Puerto Aventuras, Caleta Tankah, and the public pier at Tulum Beach. Marina Puerto Aventuras, 25 kilometres north of Tulum, is a full-service marina with fuel and provisioning. Caleta Tankah offers a sheltered launch for smaller vessels. Xpu-Ha Beach and Sian Ka'an Biosphere Reserve provide access to secluded anchorages. The port at Playa del Carmen, 60 kilometres north, handles larger yachts and crewed charters.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor Yacht (40-50 ft): Best for luxury day trips up to eight guests; from €1,500 for a full day.
  2. Catamaran (38-45 ft): Ideal for groups of up to twelve; stable cruising from €1,800 per day.
  3. Speedboat (25-30 ft): Perfect for snorkelling excursions for four people; from €500 for a half-day.
  4. Sailing Yacht (40-50 ft): Offers a quiet, wind-powered experience for six guests; from €1,200 per day.
  5. Crewed Luxury Yacht (60+ ft): Includes captain and steward; accommodates ten guests from €3,500 per day. For a detailed look, see the Fairline Targa 43 listing which typifies this category.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Tulum?

Prices vary by vessel size and duration, with shared tours from €80 per person. A half-day small motorboat for four costs around €500. A full-day catamaran charter for ten runs about €1,800. A crewed 50-foot yacht for eight guests starts at €2,500 per day. For the latest prices and availability, compare verified boat rental Tulum options on BoatHire24.

  • Shared snorkelling tour: €80, €120 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 guests): €500, €700
  • Catamaran full-day (12 guests): €1,800, €2,400
  • Crewed luxury yacht (8 guests): €2,500, €4,000 per day

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Tulum?

In Mexico, no licence is required for bareboat charter if the boat is under 10 tons and powered by an outboard. For larger yachts over 10 tons, a captain is mandatory and often included. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking. BoatHire24 advises checking with each listing for specific terms.

A local tip: book a morning departure from Marina Puerto Aventuras to avoid the afternoon trade winds, which can create choppy conditions.

When is the best season for yacht rental in Tulum?

The high season runs from December to April, with calm seas and steady north-easterly breezes. Summer months (May to October) bring higher humidity and occasional afternoon showers, but lower rates. The prevailing wind in winter is the 'norte', a cool northerly that can blow for several days. For 2026, expect peak demand during Christmas and Easter weeks; book at least three months ahead.

At a glance

  • Price range: €80, €4,000 per day
  • Peak months: December, April
  • Trip length: Half-day (4 hr) or full-day (8 hr)
  • Maximum capacity: 12 guests on a catamaran
  • Currency: EUR (€)
  • Closest major port: Marina Puerto Aventuras (25 km north)

Questions about boat rental in Tulum

Can I rent a boat in Tulum without a captain?

Yes, for small outboard-powered boats under 10 tons you can self-drive with a valid ID and a refundable deposit.

What is the closest marina to Tulum ruins?

The Caleta Tankah launch point is only 10 kilometres from the archaeological site, making it the nearest departure.

Are there all-inclusive food and drink options on charters?

Many crewed charters include an open bar and a ceviche lunch; verify the menu when booking on BoatHire24.

How far is Cozumel from Tulum by boat?

The crossing from Caleta Tankah to Cozumel is about 35 nautical miles, taking two hours by powerboat.

To explore available boats and plan your trip, browse the listings on BoatHire24. The platform simplifies comparing options and securing your charter. If you own a vessel in the region, you can list your boat to reach a global audience.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Tulum and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
